lines.english: God grants His Grace, and carries him across to the other side.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗਨਿ ਪਾਣੀ ਸਾਗਰੁ ਅਤਿ ਗਹਰਾ ਗੁਰੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਪਾਰਿ ਉਤਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: agan paanee saagar at gaharaa gur satigur paar utaaraa he |2|
lines.english: The ocean is very deep, filled with fiery water; the Guru, the True Guru, carries us across to the other side. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਬਿ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਲੇਖੁ ਨ ਮਿਟਈ ਜਮ ਦਰਿ ਅੰਧੁ ਖੁਆਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: poorab likhiaa lekh na mittee jam dar andh khuaaraa he |3|
lines.english: The primal inscription of destiny cannot be erased. The spiritually blind suffer terribly at Death's door. ||3||
The blind, self-willed manmukh does not understand.
He comes and goes in reincarnation, dying, and dying again.
Bound by their past actions, they commit sins.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਆਵਹਿ ਜਾਵਹਿ ਘਰਿ ਵਾਸੁ ਨ ਪਾਵਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: eik aaveh jaaveh ghar vaas na paaveh |
lines.english: Some come and go, and do not find a home in their own heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧੁਲੇ ਸੋਝੀ ਬੂਝ ਨ ਕਾਈ ਲੋਭੁ ਬੁਰਾ ਅਹੰਕਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: andhule sojhee boojh na kaaee lobh buraa ahankaaraa he |4|
lines.english: The blind ones have no understanding, no wisdom; they are trapped and ruined by greed and egotism. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿਰ ਬਿਨੁ ਕਿਆ ਤਿਸੁ ਧਨ ਸੀਗਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: pir bin kiaa tis dhan seegaaraa |
lines.english: Without her Husband Lord, what good are the soul-bride's decorations?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਬੇਸੁਆ ਪੂਤ ਬਾਪੁ ਕੋ ਕਹੀਐ ਤਿਉ ਫੋਕਟ ਕਾਰ ਵਿਕਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: jiau besuaa poot baap ko kaheeai tiau fokatt kaar vikaaraa he |5|
lines.english: Just as no one knows who is the father of the prostitute's son, such are the worthless, useless deeds that are done. ||5||
She has forgotten her Lord and Master, and is infatuated with another's husband.
The ghost, in the body-cage, suffers all sorts of afflictions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧਰਮ ਰਾਇ ਕੀ ਬਾਕੀ ਲੀਜੈ ਜਿਨਿ ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: dharam raae kee baakee leejai jin har kaa naam visaaraa he |6|
lines.english: The Righteous Judge of Dharma collects the balance due on the account, of those who forget the Name of the Lord. ||6||
Those who are blind to spiritual wisdom, putrefy in hell.
The scorching sun blazes with flames of poison.
The self-willed manmukh is dishonored, a beast, a demon.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਮਨਸਾ ਕੂੜੁ ਕਮਾਵਹਿ ਰੋਗੁ ਬੁਰਾ ਬੁਰਿਆਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: aasaa manasaa koorr kamaaveh rog buraa buriaaraa he |7|
lines.english: Trapped by hope and desire, he practices falsehood, and is afflicted by the terrible disease of corruption. ||7||
He carries the heavy load of sins on his forehead and head.
How can he cross the terrifying world-ocean?
The love of one's children and spouse is so sweet in this world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਬੋਹਿਥੁ ਆਦਿ ਜੁਗਾਦੀ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮਿ ਨਿਸਤਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: satigur bohith aad jugaadee raam naam nisataaraa he |8|
lines.english: From the very beginning of time, and throughout the ages, the True Guru has been the boat; through the Lord's Name, He carries us across. ||8||
The expansive expanse of the Universe is attachment to Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਮ ਕੇ ਫਾਹੇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਤੋੜੇ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਤਤੁ ਬੀਚਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: jam ke faahe satigur torre guramukh tat beechaaraa he |9|
lines.english: The True Guru snaps the noose of Death, for that Gurmukh who contemplates the essence of reality. ||9||
Cheated by falsehood, the self-willed manmukh walks along many paths;
he may be highly educated, but he burns in the fire.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਾਮੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਵਡ ਦਾਣਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪਹੁ ਸੁਖ ਸਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: amrit naam guroo vadd daanaa naam japahu sukh saaraa he |10|
lines.english: The Guru is the Great Giver of the Ambrosial Naam, the Name of the Lord. Chanting the Naam, sublime peace is obtained. ||10||
The True Guru, in His Mercy, implants Truth within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੰਡਾ ਪਾਇ ਨ ਗਡਈ ਮੂਲੇ ਜਿਸੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਰਾਖਣਹਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: kanddaa paae na gaddee moole jis satigur raakhanahaaraa he |11|
lines.english: Not even a thorn ever pierces the foot of one who has the True Guru as his Protector. ||11||
All suffering is eradicated, and one is placed on the Path.
The self-willed manmukh is like a stone slab, which is impervious to water.
Dust mixes with dust, when the body wastes away.
They live with the poisonous snake of Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਣ ਪਲਾਵ ਕਰੇ ਬਹੁਤੇਰੇ ਨਰਕਿ ਸੁਰਗਿ ਅਵਤਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: karan palaav kare bahutere narak surag avataaraa he |12|
lines.english: He cries out and weeps and wails; he is reincarnated into heaven and then hell. ||12||
This duality has ruined so many homes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਬਾਝਹੁ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਨ ਉਪਜੈ ਭਗਤਿ ਰਤੇ ਪਤੀਆਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: satigur baajhahu preet na upajai bhagat rate pateeaaraa he |13|
lines.english: Without the True Guru, love does not well up. Imbued with devotional worship, the soul is satisfied. ||13||
The faithless cynics chase after Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤ੍ਰਿਹੁ ਗੁਣ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਖਪਹਿ ਖਪਾਵਹਿ ਨਾਹੀ ਪਾਰਿ ਉਤਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: trihu gun antar khapeh khapaaveh naahee paar utaaraa he |14|
lines.english: In the three qualities, they are destroyed; they cannot cross over to the other side. ||14||
Forgetting the Naam, how can they find peace?
The false are called pigs and dogs.
They bark themselves to death; they bark and bark and howl in fear.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਿ ਤਨਿ ਝੂਠੇ ਕੂੜੁ ਕਮਾਵਹਿ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਦਰਗਹ ਹਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: man tan jhootthe koorr kamaaveh duramat daragah haaraa he |15|
lines.english: False in mind and body, they practice falsehood; through their evil-mindedness, they lose out in the Court of the Lord. ||15||
Meeting the True Guru, the mind is stabilized.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਧਨੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਅਮੋਲਕੁ ਦੇਵੈ ਹਰਿ ਜਸੁ ਦਰਗਹ ਪਿਆਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੬॥
lines.translit: har dhan naam amolak devai har jas daragah piaaraa he |16|
lines.english: They are given the priceless wealth of the Lord's Name; singing His Praises, they are His beloveds in His court. ||16||
One who seeks His Sanctuary is blessed with the Lord's Name.
